steps.title: Read the boundary line from the graph
steps.content:
The dashed line crosses the y-axis at 1. Reading two clear points on the line: (0, 1) and (1, −3). The slope is $$\frac{-3 - 1}{1 - 0} = \frac{-4}{1} = -4$$

The boundary line equation is $y = 1 - 4x$.

steps.title: Test a point in the shaded region to determine the inequality sign
steps.content:
The shaded region lies to the right of the dashed line. Pick a test point clearly inside: (2, 0).

On the boundary line at $x = 2$: $$y = 1 - 4(2) = 1 - 8 = -7$$

The test point has $y = 0$, and \$0 > -7$. Since points in the shaded region satisfy $y >$ (boundary value), the inequality is $y > 1 - 4x$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
